metadata.name: AIRL CAF Individual Types — Instance Data
metadata.domain: CyberSec / RegTech — UK NCSC CAF Audit & Advisory
metadata.description: Individual type instances for AIRL CAF platform. Pure B2B — all individuals operate within organisational hierarchy. Cross-refs: NCSC-CAF-ONT, GRC-FW-ONT.

examples.name: Chief Information Security Officer
examples.description: Senior executive accountable for information security strategy, NCSC CAF compliance, and cyber risk posture. Subclasses RRR CISO. Reports to Board/CEO on NIS Regulations, GovAssure, and cyber insurance readiness.
examples.motivations: CAF compliance, Board assurance, cyber insurance eligibility, incident response maturity, audit readiness
examples.painSummary: CAF self-assessment burden, GovAssure timeline pressure, evidencing IGP compliance, skills shortage, Board comms gap

examples.regulatoryContext: NIS Regulations 2018, GovAssure, NCSC CAF v4.0, PSN, Cyber Essentials Plus
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Evidence-driven procurement. Needs audit trail. Values pre-populated CAF mappings. Board-reportable dashboards critical.

examples.influencers: NCSC guidance, sector regulators (Ofgem/Ofcom/CQC), peer CISOs, consultancy recommendations
examples.trustSignals: NCSC-endorsed, CHECK/CREST accreditation, public sector references, UK data residency
examples.channelPreference: G-Cloud, direct procurement, Crown Commercial Service

examples.name: Security Architect / Technical Lead
examples.description: Technical specialist responsible for CAF IGP evidence, security architecture, and remediation implementation. Subclasses RRR CTO. Translates CAF requirements into technical controls.
examples.motivations: Technical accuracy, IGP evidence quality, architecture coherence, tool integration, reducing manual evidence collection
examples.painSummary: 559 IGPs to evidence, tool sprawl, evidence freshness, CAF-to-control mapping complexity

examples.regulatoryContext: NIS Regulations, NCSC CAF v4.0, Cyber Essentials, ISO 27001
examples.industrySpecificTraits: API-first mindset. Values automation. Needs CAF IGP mapping to Azure/AWS controls. Wants evidence-as-code.

examples.influencers: NCSC technical docs, vendor POCs, peer architects, security community (CIISec, ISC2)
examples.trustSignals: API documentation, CAF coverage matrix, demo environment, technical references
examples.channelPreference: Technical demo, POC, vendor direct, community recommendations

examples.name: Compliance & Governance Manager
examples.description: Manager responsible for regulatory compliance, GRC framework alignment, and audit coordination. Subclasses RRR ComplianceOfficer. Bridge between CISO strategy and operational evidence.
examples.motivations: Audit readiness, policy alignment, evidence completeness, regulator relationship, framework mapping (CAF↔ISO↔GDPR)
examples.painSummary: Multi-framework mapping, evidence duplication across CAF/ISO/GDPR, audit fatigue, policy versioning

examples.regulatoryContext: NIS Regulations, GDPR, Data Protection Act 2018, ISO 27001, DSPT (NHS)
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Spreadsheet fatigue. Values cross-framework mapping. Needs audit evidence export. Compliance calendar critical.

examples.influencers: CISO direction, regulatory deadlines, audit findings, peer compliance teams
examples.trustSignals: Framework coverage breadth, audit-ready exports, change history, role-based access
examples.channelPreference: G-Cloud, vendor demo, webinar, compliance community

examples.name: External Audit Partner / Assessor
examples.description: CHECK/CREST-accredited external assessor conducting CAF audits, GovAssure assessments, or penetration tests. Subclasses RRR Auditor. Works within audit firm/consultancy.
examples.motivations: Assessment quality, client throughput, CAF objective coverage, evidence sufficiency, consistent methodology
examples.painSummary: Evidence collection friction, client readiness variation, IGP interpretation ambiguity, report standardisation

examples.typicalBudgetRange: N/A (assessor role — client pays 15k-150k per assessment)
examples.regulatoryContext: NCSC CHECK scheme, CREST accreditation, GovAssure methodology
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Values pre-structured evidence packs. Needs secure evidence sharing. Methodology-driven. Wants re-usable assessment templates.

examples.influencers: NCSC methodology, GovAssure requirements, firm methodology, peer assessors
examples.trustSignals: CAF coverage completeness, evidence export quality, secure collaboration, audit trail
examples.channelPreference: Direct from client, firm methodology portal

examples.name: Board-Level Sponsor / SRO
examples.description: Non-executive director or Senior Responsible Owner accountable for cyber resilience at board level. Subclasses RRR CEO. Receives CISO reports, approves budgets, signs GovAssure declarations.
examples.motivations: Board assurance, NIS compliance, cyber insurance, reputational risk, personal liability under NIS Regs
examples.painSummary: Technical complexity, board-level reporting gap, understanding maturity vs compliance, insurance implications

examples.regulatoryContext: NIS Regulations (personal liability for OES), Companies Act (director duties), FRC guidance
examples.industrySpecificTraits: Needs executive summary dashboards, not technical detail. Values maturity trend lines, peer benchmarking, insurance readiness scores.

examples.influencers: CISO briefings, NED network, sector regulators, auditors, cyber insurance brokers
examples.trustSignals: Board-level reporting, peer org references, industry recognition, clear ROI narrative
examples.channelPreference: Executive briefing, CISO recommendation, board paper
